meta.setting: Ranch boundary country near French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in range work and boundary conflict

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California road-town and work texture for ranch-and-freight conflict.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Deer
research_grounding.use: Wildlife movement and forage context around fences and range edges.

image_prompt.subject: a lone fence rider kneeling beside freshly cut barbed wire while cattle drift through the gap and deer tracks cross the dust outside the fence
image_prompt.scene: late summer range under a bruised storm sky; a horse ground-tied nearby, saddle bags open with staples and pliers, a water hole glinting beyond the broken line, and mine smoke faint on the far ridge
image_prompt.composition: square clue scene with broken wire in the foreground, the rider crouched low as the working center, cattle and water hole pulling the eye toward the dispute beyond
image_prompt.camera: knee-height close three-quarter view, like an in-engine inspection scene with readable evidence and pressure behind it
material_cues: barbed wire sprung loose and catching light
bent fence staples in a dusty palm
dry yellow grass flattened by hooves
dusty chaps and sweat-dark shirt cuffs
dark saddle leather and iron pliers
storm shadow moving across the water hole
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the cut wire feel like the beginning of a fight nobody has admitted to yet. The rider should look tired enough to know who will lie about it.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine clue and range scene
render_traits: slightly simplified character and cattle silhouettes
modest texture resolution on wire, dust, leather, and grass
hard ambient occlusion under fence posts, horse, and rider
baked storm light with a dry foreground
believable 1899 range gear, saddle, pliers, and clothing
evidence-forward composition rather than posed portrait
style_modifiers.palette: dry grass gold, storm gray, dusty umber, oxidized wire, dark saddle brown, faded denim blue, and water-hole green.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable brands, labels, signs, or text. Fence marks may be scratches only.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The cut wire, rider, horse, cattle, and water hole are all fully readable.
Every visible prop and garment belongs plausibly to 1899 range work.
The image shows a boundary dispute with playable evidence.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
